steps.title: Use the trend direction
steps.content: The data trend upward, so the slope of the linear model should be positive. That eliminates the choices with negative slopes.

steps.title: Estimate the slope size
steps.content:
From the scatterplot, an increase of about $5$ in $x$ corresponds to an increase of about $9$ in $y$.
$$\frac{9}{5}=1.8$$

steps.title: Choose the matching model
steps.content:
A model with slope $0.2$ is too flat for the data. The model with positive slope close to $1.8$ is
$$y=1.80x+0.2$$
The answer is D.
